threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think asking for a 'full English' means a plate of bacon and eggs. It's a full-on adventure menu around here, mate.
Spotted a guy on the N133 humming 80s synth pop, full volume, like it was a mini disco. Brixton at midnight is always a vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy in flip-flops arguing with a pigeon over a bagel in Tempelhofer Feld—definitely a Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a late-night currywurst stand that doubles as an art gallery, with local artists showcasing their work on the food truck's sides.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively avoid the BART escalators during rush hour—too many overheated brakes to trust the trains.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, a woman on the 14 Mission started a mini sing-along, and the whole bus joined in. Just another day in SF, I guess.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always flock to the Gaudí spots but miss the hidden gems like the plaça del Sol, where real locals hang out after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still asking for a 'cortado' in English, oblivious to the fact that it’s a common word here. It’s endearing, really.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look surprised when the bus arrives 'on time'—everyone else knows it’s just a suggestion.
Last night on the 177, a guy serenaded the bus with classic 90s R&B. Half the passengers were in tears, the other half were filming. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Saw a guy setting up a pop-up café out of his bike trailer on the sidewalk, while his dog calmly watched the world go by. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how every second kebab shop has a unique sauce? It’s like a condiment Olympics while you wait for your döner.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know to always carry a backup mask for those unexpected BART smoke alarms. Tourists just seem confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni near Civic Center at rush hour, a saxophonist somehow turned the whole car into a jazz club. Crowded, but for a moment, magical.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but it's the local bars with homemade vermut that really tell our story. That's where the heart is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro ticket machines—it's always that confused look when they realize it only takes coins or a contactless card.
